fixture.description: A new HTTP handler dereferences the request body without checking it is present. A missing or malformed body throws and surfaces as an uncaught 500 instead of a 400.

grader.rubric: PASS only if the report's MAIN section flags that handleCreateNote dereferences the request body without validating it — req.body can be null and req.body.text is optional, so a missing or malformed body throws a TypeError that surfaces as an uncaught 500. Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. Credit, but do NOT require, a recommendation to validate the body and return a client error (400). FAIL if the unguarded-body / crash-on-missing-input risk is not surfaced in the main report, or only style/typing nits appear.

expected_behavior: Bugs & Security agent flags the unvalidated request body (req.body / req.body.text) as a crash / 500 on missing input
Finding severity is IMPORTANT or CRITICAL
Ideally recommends validating and returning a 400 client error
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat this as merely a typing concern to silence
Does NOT require the body line to be pre-existing to flag it
